utilization_source_quote: theoretical peak capacity ... typically about one third of that, due to inefficiencies

timeline_now.status_text: Building 1 operational. [X post](https://x.com/satyanadella/status/2044767391293509761?s=61) from Microsoft saying this date for Fairwater becoming operational.
Land clearing continues, foundation construction begins 2 of the main buildings (Building B & D). Construction also beginning on modular chiller plants.

sites.name: OpenAI Stargate Shackelford
sites.address: 175 Private Road 1604, Abilene, TX 79601, Shackelford County

timeline_now.status_text: Steel framing is underway for Buildings 1-3, and roofing is underway for Building 1.

Foundations are underway for the main buildings, and the overall layout aligns with the [architectural renderings](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) published in 2025.
Building 1 roof is complete, with air-cooled chillers in place. Building 2 and 3 roofs are partly completed. Building 4 and 5 have a foundation.
Groundwork is visible for Building 1, at the left end of the site.
Land clearing continues, foundation construction clearly begins for eastern and northern buildings (Building 1-2)
Steel framing and roofing is underway for Building 1.

sites.name: OpenAI Stargate UAE
sites.address: Nexus L&T Project Office, Al Bihouth, Al Dhafrah, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates

timeline_now.status_text: Building 1 and 2 are partly constructed. No clear evidence of more buildings starting construction.

Construction on Building 1 and 2 looks about halfway to completion. Foundations and utility trenches are visible for Building 3-4. Building 5-6 have an outline. The substation appears to be expanding.
Land clearing continues.

Since October, an array of dry cooling air-cooled condensers has been added (the array of large fans to the left of the building), and appears almost complete. Two more similar arrays appear to be under construction. More air-cooled chillers, batteries, and gas turbines have also been installed.

sites.name: Google Cedar Rapids
sites.address: 5800 EDGEWOOD RD SW CEDAR RAPIDS, IA 52404

timeline_now.status_text: Building 1 is operational. There was still a lot of work to be done on generators and cooling towers as of December, and winter will slow construction. The time from the roof being completed to operational was 7-8 months at Google Omaha, a similar data center.

The Socrates South power plant to the right of Buildings 7-11 now has 10 natural gas turbines in place, judging by the number of exhaust stacks. Battery backup has been installed nearby. To the north, a new section of tent buildings has begun east of Buildings 15-19. It is unclear how those buildings will be powered, if we assume that the Socrates North power plant is just powering Buildings 15-19.
Revision: roughly 200 MW operational by the April 2026 cutoff; the prior 590 MW April-1 milestone is treated as under construction pending a later commissioning milestone.
Buildings 12-16 are operational. Based on how long previous buildings took, when they were in a similar state to Buildings 12-16 in December.

Building 4 roof is complete and all mechanical equipment appears to be installed. However, there is still a lot of construction activity around the building. We expect another 3 months for finishing touches and commissioning before it's operational.
Backup generator pads and steel framing for chillers are visible, similar to the [3D renderings](https://www.galaxy.com/data-centers).
Roof complete on Buildings 3
Building 15 looks structurally complete.

sites.name: Amazon Madison Mega Site
sites.address: Amazon Data Services Inc, Madison Mega Site Nissan Parkway and Highway 22 Canton, Mississippi Madison County

timeline_now.status_text: Confirmation that five buildings are fully equipped with cooling and backup generators.

Building 2 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB2A is operational, presumably making up half the final capacity. CB2B was expected in March 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.

sites.name: Google New Albany
sites.address: 1101 Beech Rd SW, New Albany, OH 43054

timeline_now.status_text: Water vapor from cooling towers is present across Building 1-4, confirming their operation. Building 5 roof is still incomplete, but most backup generators and 5 cooling towers have been installed.

Building 3 has all backup generators in place but they are still being installed. Two cooling towers have fans on them, with more underway. To the south of Building 3, more land has been cleared and there's a new carpark. This suggests another building will soon start construction.
The new substation for Building 2 looks mostly complete, half of the cooling towers are installed. The black patch in the top left of the building suggests that the new roof is not quite complete.

All air-cooled chillers have been disconnected and/or removed from the site, leaving only the cooling towers for cooling. The third substation looks complete, with red cables connected to the building.
All buildings have begun construction (with groundwork starting for AI building 6), as depicted by satellite imagery.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. Activity related to the air quality permit (namely, emergency diesel generators) was reported to have started as of 2025-12-09. This is our best estimate, but note that the planned completion date in this [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2022017728) is 2026-03-01.
Confirmation that Building 3 and 4 look complete.

Building 3 has all 34 backup generators installed and 4 cooling towers installed, with 3 more cooling towers under construction. There are gaps between cooling towers suggesting that more could be added. Buildings 1 and 2 are running close to full capacity, given that water vapor is coming out of 5 of 7 cooling towers for each building. The land cleared in the southwest appears to be shaped for two more similar buildings, with notches for substations.
Major construction has finished on AI building 1, and AI building 6 has been expedited, as depicted by satellite imagery.

Building 1 operational; Roads around Building 1 complete, all cooling and side generator equipment construction has since finished as well. Although this is still earlier than the completion date reported on TDLR, based on the appearance, we believe it is operational. Reported IT power of 32 MW. [Source](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2024007561)

sites.name: Alibaba Zhangbei
sites.address: Alibaba Zhangbei Yun Calculation Data Center, Zhangbei County, Zhangjiakou, Hebei, China, 076471

To the north, a new section of tent buildings has begun east of Buildings 15-19. It is unclear how those buildings will be powered, if we assume that the Socrates North power plant is just powering Buildings 15-19.
Building 15 looks structurally complete.

Building 4 roof is complete and all mechanical equipment appears to be installed. However, there is still a lot of construction activity around the building. We expect another 3 months for finishing touches and commissioning before it's operational.

All air-cooled chillers have been disconnected and/or removed from the site, leaving only the cooling towers for cooling. The third substation looks complete, with red cables connected to the building.
